signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -15,435 (specs fading hawkish bias) vs RBNZ hike bias priced for the 27 May meeting. While BBH expects a hold, ING forecasts a hawkish stance; if RBNZ delivers a hike or signals tightening, the crowded spec short (28.0% long) faces significant unwind risk. NZD/USD at 0.5872 remains vulnerable to a short squeeze toward 0.6000 as real rates sit at -0.83%.

signals.title: CHF/NZD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Carry Divergence
signals.text: CHF outperformance (avg +0.530%) vs NZD weakness (avg -0.360%) creates a 0.889% G8 spread, triggering a bilateral theme alert. CHF strength persists despite a -225bp carry disadvantage vs NZD (SNB 0% vs RBNZ 2.25%), signaling non-carry flows or safe-haven demand dominating the cross. NZD/CHF at 0.4586 reflects this divergence ahead of the RBNZ decision.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Intervention Risk + Options Skew
signals.text: USD/JPY at 158.94 holds just below the 160.00 critical intervention threshold. While the +278bp OIS carry (SOFR vs TONA) supports the pair, the options market is pricing downside protection with a -1.22 moderate put skew (25d RR). HSBC notes intervention requires BoJ support to be sustainable; watch for verbal intervention or BoJ TONA repricing to catalyze a move toward 155.00.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Volatility Premium + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD IV at 13.8% vs HV30 at 8.9% generates a +4.9% Vol Risk Premium, signaling heavy institutional hedging. This aligns with a retail/institutional divergence: retail is 72% short (fading the move) while COT LF remains net long +57,180 (74.5% long). Despite a 2.60σ unemployment rate shock, the 84bp AUD OIS carry advantage keeps the pair bid at 0.7171.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Volatility Premium + CESI Support
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1647 shows an elevated VRP of +2.2% (IV 7.1% vs HV30 4.9%), indicating the market is paying up for protection despite a neutral 25d RR (-0.39). While BBH warns of downside to 1.1400, the EUR CESI (net +5) and USD CESI (net +24) both show data outperforming expectations. The 158bp OIS carry advantage for USD remains the structural ceiling for EUR upside.
